diagrams.title: Cache Memory Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows CPU at top connecting to cache, which connects to main memory. Arrows show data flow and directions.
diagrams.purpose: Understanding where cache fits in memory hierarchy and data flow path

diagrams.title: 4-Way Set-Associative Cache Structure
diagrams.description: Rows represent sets (0 to N), columns represent 4 ways. Each cell contains tag, valid bit, dirty bit, and data.
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how set-associative cache maps addresses and organizes lines

diagrams.title: LRU Counter Mechanism
diagrams.description: Shows 4 counters (0-3) tracking access order. When way 2 accessed, other counters increment, way 2 becomes 0.

diagrams.title: Cache Hit/Miss Flowchart
diagrams.description: Decision tree: CPU requests address → Check valid bit and tag match → Hit: return data, Miss: fetch from memory → Update cache → Return data
diagrams.purpose: Understanding cache access sequence and decisions
